title: Proof Boundary — Self-Healing Systems Need Governance
supported_claims: Automated remediation changes operational authority because the system can modify production conditions without immediate human action.
Governed self-healing requires action boundaries, approval rules, escalation, rollback, observability, and accountable ownership.
Trust depends on preserving evidence about what was detected, changed, and whether the remediation succeeded.
unsupported_claims: Any product-specific self-healing capability not revalidated against current primary documentation.
Any quantified claim about incident-duration or operational-cost reduction.
Any assertion that autonomous remediation is appropriate for every failure class.
